paper_title: Induced 2-degenerate Subgraphs of Triangle-free Planar Graphs
summary: Conjecture 1.1 from arXiv:1709.04036 — that every triangle-free planar graph contains an induced 2-degenerate subgraph on at least 7/8 of its vertices — remains open. The source paper itself provides the best known partial progress: an unconditional 4/5 bound (Theorem 1.2) and a 7/8n − 18n_3 bound (Theorem 1.3) where n_3 is the number of low-degree vertices. A broad web search across subsequent literature (through May 2026) found no paper proving or disproving the full 7/8 bound.
search_queries: induced 2-degenerate subgraph triangle-free planar graph 7/8 vertices Dvořák Kelly conjecture
arxiv 1709.04036 citing papers induced degenerate planar graph
notes: No follow-up paper resolving the conjecture was found. Related work on induced k-degenerate subgraphs of planar graphs (e.g., arXiv:2002.07984 on 3-degenerate subgraphs, arXiv:2511.13693 on sparse graphs) cites the 4/5 bound from this paper but does not improve the 2-degenerate triangle-free planar bound. The conjecture is tight for the 3-cube graph (unique 3-regular triangle-free planar graph on 8 vertices).
